MELSubItem.Title: Flight Deck to Cabin, Cabin to Flight Deck Functions

ListItem.Para: Flight deck to cabin and cabin to flight deck interphone functions operate normally on at least 50% of the cabin handsets.
Flight deck to cabin and cabin to flight deck interphone functions operate normally on at least one handset at each pair of exit doors.
Alternate communications procedures between affected flight attendant’s station(s) and flight deck are established and used.

Note.Para: Any function that operates normally may be used.

ListItem.Para: Prior to flight, the pilot in command must brief Purser on the unserviceablity and advise that crew members adjacent to the unserviceable interphone must pass communication to/from the affected crew member as required.
The PA may also be utilised as required to advise affected crew members of urgent information.

Note.Para: For cabin interphone equipment associated with the observer seats, the airplane must be dispatched using MEL item 25-11-02.

ListItem.Para: Cabin to cabin interphone functions operate normally on at least 50% of the cabin handsets.
Cabin to cabin interphone functions operate normally on at least one handset at each pair of exit doors.
Alternate communications procedures between affected flight attendant’s station(s) are established and used.

Note.Para: Any function that operates normally may be used.

ListItem.Para: Prior to flight, the pilot-in-command must brief cabin crew members on the inoperative function(s). Communications procedures between the affected flight attendants station(s) may include relay of message through the nearest operative handsets, the use of hand signals and voice as appropriate..
Passenger Address system may also be used.

MELSubItem.Title: Flight Deck to Ground/Ground to Flight Deck Function (Includes Nose Gear Flight Interphone Jack)

DispatchCondition.Title: Nose gear Service Interphone Jack Operates Normally

ListItem.Para: Nose gear service interphone jack operates normally.
Alternate procedures are established and used.

OperationProcedure.Para: Use flight interphone for communication with ground crew. In the event of a failure of the Service Interphone system after both engines are started, marshalling signals shall be used as an alternate cockpit to ground communication procedure. Refer to Lido General Parts (Lido mPilot)-1. General Part—1.3 Aerodromes-1.3.2 Aerodrome Information Signs and Signals—1.3.2.3 Marshalling Signals -1.3.2.3.2 Pilot to Signalman.
Position Overhead panel SERV INTPH switch ON to connect service and flight interphone systems.

DispatchCondition.Title: Nose Gear Service Interphone Jack Inoperative

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are established and used.

OperationProcedure.Para: In the event of a failure of the Service Interphone system after both engines are started, marshalling signals shall be used as an alternate cockpit to ground communication procedure.
Refer to Lido General Parts (Lido mPilot)-1. General Part—1.3 Aerodromes-1.3.2 Aerodrome Information Signs and Signals—1.3.2.3 Marshalling Signals -1.3.2.3.2 Pilot to Signalman.
Position Overhead panel SERV INTPH switch ON to connect service and flight interphone systems.
